design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore towns and world to recruit allies, engage in grid-based tactical battles, level up characters through EXP, promote units at level 20 to unlock new classes, progress through story by defeating key enemy commanders
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management between exploration and combat; strategic positioning on grid-based battlefields; unit class progression choices with branching promotion paths
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a growing army of diverse characters with unique personalities and backgrounds; lead a tactical force as the Shining Force leader (Bowie); uncover a world-spanning mystery while gathering allies
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Commitment to free-roaming exploration without chapter restrictions; non-linear ally recruitment with hidden characters requiring specific events; dual promotion paths for certain units (one requiring hidden items); emphasis on character personality and background in a tactical framework; losses result in full battle retry from town rather than game-over, encouraging experimentation

difficulty.continues_system: Defeat in battle (Bowie defeated or withdraws) returns player to nearest town; can recover allies and retry same battle
difficulty.checkpointing: Town-based checkpoint system; player returns to nearest town on battle loss
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through team composition (recruiting specific hidden allies), class promotion choices (including hidden promotion paths), equipment management, and tactical positioning on grid battlefields

progression.upgrade_system: Level-based EXP system; units gain levels through combat and various actions; class-based ability trees tied to unit type (defines weapon types and spell availability); equipment system with visible and hidden treasures throughout world
progression.unlock_mechanism: Character promotion at level 20 unlocks new classes; some characters have two promotion paths, with one requiring special hidden items; hidden allies unlock through specific story events; treasures (visible and hidden) unlock equipment
replayability_hooks: Multiple promotion paths for certain characters (especially those with hidden second class option)
Hidden allies requiring specific events to recruit
Visible and hidden treasures scattered throughout explorable world
Free-roaming without chapter system allows revisiting areas in different sequence
Dual class promotion system encourages experimentation with character builds

world_building_techniques: Environmental exploration of towns and locales with NPC interaction
Character backgrounds and personalities for each ally unit
Headquarters locations where player inspects and talks with allies
Possession mechanics revealing true threat (Zeon demon king)
Multi-kingdom setting with political conflict (Granseal vs. Galam)
Series continuity with Shining Force Gaiden: Final Conflict (40-70 years prior)
Incremental plot revelation through exploration and dialogue

boss_design.philosophy: Key battles driven by story progression; enemy commanders serve as critical defeat conditions; boss encounters tied to possession mechanic and multi-stage villain arc (Gizmo possessions, King Galam possessed, final confrontation with Zeon)
notable_bosses: Possessed King Galam (holds Princess Elis hostage at Ancient Tower)
Zeon (demon king unsealed by jewel theft; main antagonist)

faction_indicators: Kingdom of Granseal (player base, under siege)
Kingdom of Galam (invaded by demon forces, politically conflicted)
Thief Slade faction (joins party after dungeon escape)
danger_signals: Unsealing of Zeon through jewel theft at shrine
King Granseal's unexplained sickness and possession
Cross-kingdom military invasion (Galam forces capture party)
Collapse of ground around Ancient Tower
Forced resettlement of Granseal populace to Parmecia continent
environmental_storytelling: Granseal Castle under investigation after king's possession
Kingdom dungeon holding captured party members
Ancient Tower structure collapsing after boss defeat
Forced continental migration indicating scale of threat
Granseal's flourishing nation on new continent (sequel continuity)
